items.title: Manage Vendor Risk in a Few Practical Steps
items.author: Daniel Nutkis
items.content: <article>Risk tolerance, exposure visibility, board oversight — handling third-party risk is complicated but achievable with disciplined, precise governance.</article>

items.title: Frontier AI: The Genie's Out of the Bottle, but Where's the Rulebook?
items.author: Arielle Waldman
items.content: <article>Cutting-edge artificial intelligence models are deploying with more independence and less human oversight. Several state governments are trying to legislate transparency in their use.</article>

items.title: ClickFix's Mushrooming Ecosystem Demands New Defense Tactics
items.author: Elizabeth Montalbano
items.content: <article>The attack vector is available for rent at scale, and evades AV and EDR, leaving YARA analysis as the best detection option.</article>

items.title: Cursor IDE Auto-Executes Malicious Code in Poisoned Repos
items.author: Alexander Culafi
items.content: <article>Researchers reported the vulnerability to Cursor in December, but it still remains in the popular AI coding platform and can be exploited in poisoned repository attacks.</article>

items.title: Weak Security Continues to Fuel Russian Cyberattacks
items.author: Jai Vijayan
items.content: <article>In a first, the UK and the EU jointly imposed sanctions on Russian individuals and entities for cyberattacks and disinformation campaigns in the region.</article>

items.title: 'Yellow Teams' Are Defining the Future of AI Security
items.author: Nate Nelson
items.content: <article>In some companies, engineers are building defense and attack tools to test the potential of artificial intelligence for cybersecurity — and its threat.</article>

items.title: GigaWiper Lets Threat Actors Choose Their Own Destructive Attack
items.author: Elizabeth Montalbano
items.content: <article>A modular implant borrows from various malware families to combine both backdoor and wiper activities to maximize impact and minimize operational output.</article>

items.title: Turning the Tables on Email Scammers With 'ScamBuster'
items.author: Elizabeth Montalbano
items.content: <article>An open source, AI-driven system adopts victim personas to engage with phishing attackers, allowing organizations and law enforcement to gather relevant data on cybercriminal operations.</article>

items.title: Jen Ellis: Connecting Cyber Community With Political Machinery
items.author: Ericka Chickowski
items.content: <article>Security Pro File: On the heels of her recent honors as a Member of the Order of the British Empire (MBE), we take a look back at the events that shaped Jen Ellis' advocacy on behalf of security researchers.</article>

items.title: Cybercriminals Flock to Healthcare Businesses as Attacks Surge
items.author: Robert Lemos
items.content: <article>While cyberattacks against hospitals and clinics grew modestly in the first half of 2026, attacks on service providers and other healthcare businesses more than doubled.</article>

items.title: Fresh ATM Crypto Software Bugs: Jackpot or Bust?
items.author: Nate Nelson
items.content: <article>Organizations, and possibly ATMs, are at risk of compromise, thanks to holes in a Microsoft BitLocker security wrapper.</article>

items.title: More Countries Jump on the Social Media 'Ban Wagon'
items.author: Arielle Waldman
items.content: <article>Age restrictions on accounts may be more of a stopgap because industry compliance is already falling short. Tech giants are struggling to follow the laws without affecting users.</article>

items.title: AI Coding: Do Security Risks Outweigh Productivity Gains?
items.author: Alexander Culafi
items.content: <article>AI coding tools cost $19-$200/month/user, but security scanning, remediation, and false positives add hidden costs. Are the productivity gains worth it?</article>

items.title: Iran's Cyber Crosshairs Focus Beyond Critical Infrastructure
items.author: Joe Slowik
items.content: <article>Obscurity isn't a defense. If your company has any Internet-facing vulnerability, you're at risk from multiple threats.</article>

items.title: Microsoft Reins in RoguePlanet Zero-Day Threat
items.author: Rob Wright
items.content: <article>The researcher known as "Nightmare-Eclipse" published a proof-of-concept (PoC) exploit for the Windows Defender vulnerability in early June after dropping several other Microsoft zero-days.</article>

items.title: AI Agents Are a New Kind of Identity — and Most Organizations Aren't Ready
items.author: Mora Gozani
items.content: <article>If you're handling AI agents like a service account or API token, consider yourself behind. AI agents need a fundamentally different approach.</article>

items.title: As Global Conflicts Go Digital, Businesses Need Wartime Game Plans
items.author: Nate Nelson
items.content: <article>The fate of a Ukrainian tax software company shows how modern cyber warfare can claim casualties far beyond the battlefield, and how businesses across the ocean still need to protect themselves.</article>

items.title: AI Gateways Offer Attackers the Keys to the Kingdom
items.author: Jai Vijayan
items.content: <article>A cryptomining incident highlights how AI gateways can provide access to AI models, cloud infrastructure, and identity and access management (IAM) data.</article>

items.title: 'GodDamn' Ransomware Uses BYOVD to Smite US Companies
items.author: Nate Nelson
items.content: <article>Microsoft signed a malicious kernel driver, and now it's being used to kill security software in ransomware attacks.</article>

items.title: European Organizations Have a Collaboration Security Confidence Gap
items.author: Jai Vijayan
items.content: <article>A new survey shows security leaders have an inflated sense of safety regarding their collaboration tools and platforms.</article>

items.title: Mexico's New Cyber Plan Faces Its First Real Test
items.author: Robert Lemos
items.content: <article>The Latin American nation's cybersecurity plan — still in the expansion phase — has to survive its own knockout round during the FIFA World Cup.</article>

items.title: Lone Attacker Uses AI to Breach AWS Cloud Environment in 72 Hours
items.author: Alexander Culafi
items.content: <article>The attacker exploited AI workflows, chained cloud weaknesses, and stolen credentials to extort a large Amazon customer.</article>

items.title: Vidar Infostealer Hammers SMBs via Malvertising Campaign
items.author: Elizabeth Montalbano
items.content: <article>A financially motivated operation uses lures of cracked or pirated software to deliver a two-for-one malware combo for data theft and cryptomining.</article>

items.title: State IDs for AI Agents: Will Estonia Set a Precedent?
items.author: Nate Nelson
items.content: <article>The world's digital testing ground plans to help people use AI agents for government purposes.</article>

items.title: Big Brand Jobs Scam Targets Marketing Pros' Google Accounts
items.author: Rob Wright
items.content: <article>The phishing campaign uses several tactics, including nested redirects, to evade detection and steal credentials from unsuspecting targets.</article>

items.title: Dialogflow CX 'Rogue Agent' Flaw Enabled AI Chatbot Data Theft
items.author: Alexander Culafi
items.content: <article>Varonis reported the flaw to Google in late 2025 and it has been addressed, but it reminds defenders to take a fresh look at their AI Infrastructure security.</article>

items.title: 'GitLost' Flaw Leaks Private Data From GitHub's Agentic Workflows
items.author: Elizabeth Montalbano
items.content: <article>The flaw allows an unauthenticated attacker to craft a GitHub Issue in an org's public repository and then silently pull data from its private repos, too.</article>

items.title: 'BusySnake' Infostealer Slithers Into Critical Infrastructure Networks
items.author: Jai Vijayan
items.content: <article>A threat group researchers call "Armored Likho" has gained access to government agencies and electrical power entities in Russia, Brazil, and Kazakhstan.</article>

items.title: CitrixBleed-ing Again? NetScaler Vulnerability Under Attack
items.author: Rob Wright
items.content: <article>Attackers wasted little time targeting the latest memory disclosure flaw in Citrix's NetScaler products, after researchers published a proof-of-concept exploit (PoC).</article>

items.title: JadePuffer: The First Complete LLM-Driven Ransomware Attack
items.author: Elizabeth Montalbano
items.content: <article>An "agentic threat actor" successfully exploited a Langflow flaw to steal data from a production database server and encrypt other systems.</article>

items.title: Chinese LLMs Broaden the Gap Between Attackers & Defenders
items.author: Robert Lemos
items.content: <article>Two new models from Chinese firms compete with top US mainstream and frontier models. Should cyber-defenders be worried?</article>

items.title: Aussies Face Reduced Cybercrime Risk, as Pressure Shifts to SMBs
items.author: Nate Nelson
items.content: <article>Improved institutional safeguards and stricter regulations have pushed the burdens of protection and risk reduction on to Australian businesses.</article>

items.title: Apple Reverses Age-Old Patch Policy to Keep Up With AI
items.author: Nate Nelson
items.content: <article>Expect more compressed patching cycles from Apple going forward, as attackers leverage artificial intelligence to reduce time to exploit.</article>

items.title: FortiBleed Actors Collaborating With Inc, Lynx Ransomware Gangs
items.author: Rob Wright
items.content: <article>After gaining a foothold in thousands of Fortinet firewalls, the attackers are starting to monetize that access, and are also piling on a Nextcloud zero-day bug.</article>

items.title: Ransomware Thugs Masquerade as Interpol to Entice Small Biz
items.author: Jai Vijayan
items.content: <article>The ransomware campaign relies on basic social engineering and stretches across multiple regions, including the US, Europe, Middle East, and elsewhere.</article>

items.title: Anthropic's AI Finds Bugs. IBM Bets $5B It Can Fix Them.
items.author: Jeffrey Schwartz
items.content: <article>IBM and Red Hat assign 20,000 engineers to the new Project Lightwell service as Anthropic's Mythos findings ignite debate over how to secure the open source software supply chain.</article>

items.title: Crafty Phishing Campaigns Auto-Adapt to Victim's Device, OS
items.author: Alexander Culafi
items.content: <article>Attackers fingerprint victims through user-agent data to deliver OS-specific payloads, increasing compromise rates and campaign profitability.</article>

items.title: And the Winner in Dominant Malware Delivery? ClickFix
items.author: Rob Wright
items.content: <article>Researchers say the highly effective social engineering technique is no longer the exception for malware attacks — it's now the rule.</article>

items.title: When Too Much Security Data Becomes the Risk
items.author: Joan Goodchild
items.content: <article>Rapid growth turned routine firewall logs into a security and budget liability. One CISO used artificial intelligence to filter what data truly belongs in the SIEM.</article>

items.title: 'Phantom Squatting': An Emerging AI-Driven Supply Chain Threat
items.author: Elizabeth Montalbano
items.content: <article>LLMs consistently hallucinate Web domains for legitimate brands that attackers can register for malicious activity in a difficult-to-detect attack vector.</article>

items.title: Safe Events Start With Threat Intel & Digital Security
items.author: Olga Polishchuk
items.content: <article>Planning ahead to defend against cyber threats is the work that keeps events uneventful.</article>

items.title: China-Linked Group Targets Southeast Asia Critical Systems
items.author: Robert Lemos
items.content: <article>The group compromised at least 10 regional organizations, including two state-owned entities, and deployed a new backdoor.</article>

items.title: Fake Bug Report Hijacks AI Coding Agents at Scale
items.author: Jai Vijayan
items.content: <article>"Agentjacking" is the latest demonstration of how easily attackers can exploit an AI agent's inability to differentiate between content and instructions.</article>

items.title: Attackers Seize Exposed AI Endpoints to Power Offensive Ops
items.author: Alexander Culafi
items.content: <article>Threat actors don't need any special authentication to reach a target endpoint — they just need to know where it is.</article>

items.title: Why Identity Security Is Your Cyber Career Entry Point
items.author: Kristina Beek
items.content: <article>In this "Heard it From a CISO" video, Silverfort CISO John Paul Cunningham explains that AI in cybersecurity workflows is creating opportunities rather than eliminating jobs — and there are more ways than ever to break into this essential field.</article>

items.title: Phishers Gain Persistence at EU, Asia Hospitality Orgs
items.author: Elizabeth Montalbano
items.content: <article>Separate but similar campaigns described by Microsoft and Trend Micro use malicious zip files to spread malware via social engineering and obsfucation, including blockchain abuse.</article>

items.title: AI-Generated Workflows Are a Silent Security Disaster
items.author: Yelena Mujibur Sheikh
items.content: <article>Teams are dealing with a truly dangerous problem — automation that works, but that no one understands.</article>

items.title: NIST Enrichment Reductions Impact CVE Coverage, Accuracy
items.author: Rob Wright
items.content: <article>The National Institute of Standards and Technology (NIST) scaled back the number of CVEs it selects for in-depth analysis, but the move has produced mixed results, according to researchers.</article>

items.title: 'Djinn' Stealer Targets Cloud, AI Credentials
items.author: Jai Vijayan
items.content: <article>The infostealer was delivered via CVE-2026-48558, a critical authentication bypass vulnerability in SimpleHelp, targeting credentials linking development and admin environments to wider enterprise systems.</article>

items.title: Vulnerabilities Expose Private Data in Indian Government Systems
items.author: Nate Nelson
items.content: <article>One critical vulnerability, among many discovered by a researcher, could have allowed anyone to walk in and take over a national government portal.</article>
